title: leontopodium artemisiifolium
intro: Leontopodium artemisiifolium, also known as Edelweiss, is a flowering plant native to the alpine regions of Europe. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. Edelweiss has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive issues and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and its extracts are also used in skincare products to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
